column2: Distinguish between endocrine and nervous system signaling based on speed, duration, and target specificity
Classify hormones into families (steroid, peptide, amine) and explain how molecular structure determines receptor specificity
Analyze negative feedback loops to predict how changes in hormone levels trigger compensatory responses
Map the hypothalamic-pituitary-target gland axis and explain the three-level feedback mechanism
Apply insulin and glucagon signaling to predict blood glucose regulation in fed and fasted states
Evaluate the relationship between insulin sensitivity, lifestyle factors, and metabolic health
Trace the thyroid axis and explain how T4-to-T3 conversion regulates metabolic rate
Compare adrenaline and cortisol as fast and slow stress responses and predict their tissue effects
Explain the HPG axis and describe how testosterone and estradiol are produced and regulated in all sexes
Evaluate estradiol's roles across bone, brain, and connective tissue health independent of reproductive function
Distinguish ghrelin and leptin signaling and analyze how each regulates short-term and long-term appetite
Describe growth hormone release patterns and apply sleep and training principles to optimize anabolic signaling
Analyze hormonal cross-talk to predict how one axis influences the sensitivity or output of another
Evaluate sleep, stress, nutrition, and training as primary levers that move hormonal outputs across all systems
Create an integrated hormonal profile connecting multiple axes and predict system-wide responses to lifestyle interventions
